'''Scott Anderson''' (born 8 January 1986) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000 and 2010s. He played for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] of the [[Super League]]. A {{rlp|pr}}, he previously played for the [[Melbourne Storm]]. He also played for the [[Brisbane Broncos]].

==Playing career==
===Melbourne Storm===
Anderson played from the interchange bench in Melbourne's [[2008 NRL Grand Final]] loss to [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]].<ref name="Grand final: As it happened">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24449388-23214,00.html|title=Grand final: As it happened|publisher=Fox Sports|date=2008-10-05|access-date=2008-10-05}}</ref> The following season he played in the [[2009 NRL Grand Final]] victory over the [[Parramatta Eels]], again from the bench.  This premiership was later stripped in 2010 due to the club's multiple and deliberate breaches of the salary cap.<ref name="2009 NRL Grand Final">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/telstrapremiership/telstrapremiershipscores/tabid/10240/roundid/833/fixtureid/5801/infotabid/3/default.aspx|title=2009 NRL Grand Final|publisher=National Rugby League|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091003020642/http://www.nrl.com/telstrapremiership/telstrapremiershipscores/tabid/10240/roundid/833/fixtureid/5801/infotabid/3/default.aspx|archive-date=3 October 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/storm/they-are-ours-why-cameron-smith-will-always-recognise-storms-stripped-premierships/news-story/6397becb6e5091d6804ef91c578c56bd/|title='They are ours': Why Cameron Smith will always recognise Storm's stripped premierships|publisher=Fox Sports}}</ref>

===Brisbane Broncos===
On 12 November 2009 it was announced he had signed with the [[Brisbane Broncos]] on a two-year deal.<ref name="Anderson heads north to Broncos">{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,26338855-5018866,00.html|title=Anderson heads north to Broncos|publisher=Fox Sports|date=2009-11-12|access-date=2009-11-12}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity Wildcats===
Anderson signed with the Wildcats in 2013, joining the club for the [[Super League XIX|2014 season]], originally on a one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web |title=Bronco Scott Anderson joins Wakefield |url=https://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/bronco-scott-anderson-joins-wakefield/bryad7m8a |website=sbs.com.au |publisher=[[Special Broadcasting Service]] |access-date=7 April 2022 |date=26 November 2013}}</ref>

He retired from rugby league at the end of the 2016 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Scott Anderson: Wakefield Wildcats prop announces retirement |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/37306364 |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=7 April 2022 |date=8 September 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=St Helens v Wakefield Trinity: Scott Anderson keen to end Trinity spell on a high |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-v-wakefield-trinity-scott-anderson-keen-end-trinity-spell-high-1790833 |website=The Yorkshire Post |access-date=7 April 2022 |date=23 September 2016}}</ref>
